title: Acute Lethal Hepato-Bronchial Fistula: Emergency Perioperative Management Using Two Single-Lumen Endotracheal Tubes for Lung Isolation
author: Olumuyiwa A., Bamgbade
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Background: Hepato-bronchial fistula (HBF) is a rare complication of hepatic abscess. The usual atypical presentation  poses diagnostic and management challenges to clinicians.   Case Presentation: This is a case report of acute lethal HBF in an adult who underwent successful surgical   management at a hospital in Nigeria. Anaesthetic management was complicated by the lack of appropriate   double-lumen endotracheal tubes or bronchial blockers for lung isolation. Therefore, two small cuffed ID=5mm   endotracheal tubes (ETT) were used for lung isolation, with a good outcome.   Conclusion: This report highlights the safe option of using two small ETTs for perioperative lung isolation, especially in  resource-limited settings. The report describes the challenging and meticulous perioperative management of HBF.
